cuisine.display:
{ "cooking": { "cuisine": { "type": { "italian": "# Italian Cuisine

Italian cooking centers on high-quality, seasonal ingredients prepared simply to let flavors shine.

## Key Principles
- **Simplicity** — Most dishes use 4–8 ingredients. The quality of each ingredient matters more than complexity.
- **Regionality** — Northern Italy favors butter, rice, and polenta; Southern Italy uses olive oil, tomatoes, and dried pasta.
- **Al dente** — Pasta should be cooked firm to the bite. Start tasting 2 minutes before the package time.
- **Soffritto** — The holy trinity of onion, carrot, and celery, slowly sweated in olive oil, forms the base of countless dishes.

## Essential Techniques
- **Emulsifying pasta water** — Reserve starchy pasta water and toss with sauce to create a silky coating.
- **Risotto stirring** — Constant stirring releases starch for a creamy texture without cream.
- **Bruschetta method** — Rub toasted bread with raw garlic, then drizzle with good olive oil.

## Staple Ingredients
Olive oil, San Marzano tomatoes, Parmigiano-Reggiano, fresh basil, garlic, dried pasta, arborio rice, prosciutto, mozzarella.

## Classic Dishes
Cacio e pepe, carbonara, margherita pizza, risotto alla milanese, osso buco, tiramisu." } } } }

}

code:
var cookingSteps = {
  steps: [
    {
      stepNumber: 1,
      action: "Cook spaghetti in boiling salted water",
      technique: "boil",
      duration: "8-10 minutes",
      temperature: "rolling boil",
      donenessCue: "Pasta is al dente (firm to the bite, slightly resistant when chewed)",
      tip: "Reserve 1 cup of pasta water before draining - the starchy water helps create a silky sauce"
    },
    {
      stepNumber: 2,
      action: "Heat olive oil in large skillet over medium-low heat",
      technique: "heat",
      duration: "1-2 minutes",
      temperature: "medium-low",
      donenessCue: "Oil is warm and shimmering but not smoking",
      tip: "Keep heat low to prevent garlic from burning - burned garlic is bitter"
    },
    {
      stepNumber: 3,
      action: "Add sliced garlic to warm oil and cook slowly",
      technique: "sauté",
      duration: "3-4 minutes",
      temperature: "medium-low",
      donenessCue: "Garlic is lightly golden and fragrant, not brown",
      tip: "Stir constantly to ensure even cooking - golden garlic, not brown"
    },
    {
      stepNumber: 4,
      action: "Add red pepper flakes to oil and garlic",
      technique: "infuse",
      duration: "30 seconds",
      temperature: "medium-low",
      donenessCue: "Red pepper flakes are sizzling and aroma is released",
      tip: "This infuses the oil with heat and flavor - dont skip this step"
    },
    {
      stepNumber: 5,
      action: "Add drained spaghetti to skillet with garlic oil",
      technique: "toss",
      duration: "2 minutes",
      temperature: "medium-low",
      donenessCue: "Pasta is evenly coated with oil and garlic mixture",
      tip: "Use tongs to toss pasta gently - this coats each strand evenly"
    },
    {
      stepNumber: 6,
      action: "Add reserved pasta water, 2 tablespoons at a time",
      technique: "emulsify",
      duration: "1-2 minutes",
      temperature: "medium-low",
      donenessCue: "Sauce becomes creamy and clings to pasta",
      tip: "Pasta water contains starch that creates a silky emulsion - add gradually until sauce coats pasta"
    },
    {
      stepNumber: 7,
      action: "Season with salt and pepper to taste",
      technique: "season",
      duration: "30 seconds",
      temperature: "medium-low",
      donenessCue: "Taste and adjust seasoning as needed",
      tip: "Start with less salt - you can always add more, but you cant remove it"
    },
    {
      stepNumber: 8,
      action: "Remove from heat and stir in chopped parsley",
      technique: "fold",
      duration: "30 seconds",
      temperature: "off heat",
      donenessCue: "Parsley is evenly distributed and still bright green",
      tip: "Fresh parsley adds brightness and color - add at the end to preserve freshness"
    }
  ],
  totalActiveTime: "15 minutes",
  totalPassiveTime: "5 minutes"
}
completeTask("recipe", "cook-step-by-step", cookingSteps)

plan.description: Generates a complete recipe with ingredients, technique guidance, and nutritional breakdown

tasks.instructions: Based on the user's request and knowledge context (cuisine type, dietary restrictions, preferred cooking techniques), determine the appropriate cuisine and recommend a specific dish. Consider the occasion, number of servings, available time, and dietary needs. Load the relevant cuisine knowledge to inform the recommendation. Suggest a dish that matches the user's skill level and available equipment.

description.description: Brief description of the dish and its origin

rationale.description: Why this dish fits the user's context and preferences

tasks.instructions: Based on the selected dish, generate a complete ingredient list organized by category. Include precise quantities for the specified number of servings. For each ingredient that may conflict with dietary restrictions, provide a substitution. Flag any allergens. Use the scaleIngredients function if the user needs to adjust servings. Consider seasonal availability and suggest alternatives for hard-to-find items.

servingsBase.description: Number of servings this list is scaled for

pantryAssumptions.description: Common ingredients assumed to be on hand (salt, pepper, oil, etc.)

tasks.instructions: Create a detailed mise en place guide for the selected dish. Organize all preparation tasks that should be completed before cooking begins — washing, chopping, measuring, marinating, pre-heating. Group tasks by timing: what can be done hours ahead, what should be done right before cooking. Include knife cuts, sizes, and any technique notes for each preparation step.

details.description: Specific cut, size, or technique notes

timeAhead.description: How far in advance this can be done

advancePrep.description: Tasks that can be done hours or a day ahead

immediatePrep.description: Tasks to do right before cooking

equipmentNeeded.description: Tools and equipment to have ready

tasks.instructions: Generate the full cooking instructions for the dish. Load the relevant technique knowledge (sauté, grill, braise) to provide accurate method guidance. Each step should include precise timing, temperature, and sensory cues for doneness (visual, aroma, texture). Flag critical moments where timing or technique matters most. Include tips for common mistakes and how to recover from them.

donenessCue.description: How to know this step is complete (visual, aroma, texture)

tip.description: Common mistake or pro tip for this step

totalPassiveTime.description: Total resting, simmering, or baking time

tasks.instructions: Analyze the nutritional content of the completed recipe. Use the estimateCalories function to calculate calorie totals from macronutrient estimates. Break down per-serving values for calories, protein, carbohydrates, fat, and fiber. Identify the primary nutrient sources from the ingredient list. Note any nutritional highlights or considerations relevant to the user's dietary restrictions.

dietaryNotes.description: Highlights or considerations for specific dietary needs

output.description: A simple yet elegant Roman pasta dish with garlic and olive oil, perfect for beginners
output.rationale: Spaghetti Aglio e Olio is ideal for beginners as it requires minimal ingredients, teaches fundamental Italian techniques like proper pasta cooking and garlic infusion, and accommodates the vegetarian dietary restriction

advancePrep.details: Slice into thin rounds or mince finely for even flavor distribution
advancePrep.timeAhead: Up to 1 hour before cooking (keep covered to prevent drying)
Up to 2 hours before cooking (store in damp paper towel in fridge)

immediatePrep.details: Have ready in small bowl or measuring cup
Adjust quantity based on spice preference
Large pot with plenty of water - use at least 4 quarts

steps.action: Cook spaghetti in boiling salted water
steps.donenessCue: Pasta is al dente (firm to the bite, slightly resistant when chewed)
steps.tip: Reserve 1 cup of pasta water before draining - the starchy water helps create a silky sauce

steps.action: Heat olive oil in large skillet over medium-low heat
steps.donenessCue: Oil is warm and shimmering but not smoking
steps.tip: Keep heat low to prevent garlic from burning - burned garlic is bitter

steps.action: Add sliced garlic to warm oil and cook slowly
steps.donenessCue: Garlic is lightly golden and fragrant, not brown
steps.tip: Stir constantly to ensure even cooking - golden garlic, not brown

steps.action: Add red pepper flakes to oil and garlic
steps.donenessCue: Red pepper flakes are sizzling and aroma is released
steps.tip: This infuses the oil with heat and flavor - dont skip this step

steps.action: Add drained spaghetti to skillet with garlic oil
steps.donenessCue: Pasta is evenly coated with oil and garlic mixture
steps.tip: Use tongs to toss pasta gently - this coats each strand evenly

steps.action: Add reserved pasta water, 2 tablespoons at a time
steps.donenessCue: Sauce becomes creamy and clings to pasta
steps.tip: Pasta water contains starch that creates a silky emulsion - add gradually until sauce coats pasta

steps.action: Season with salt and pepper to taste
steps.donenessCue: Taste and adjust seasoning as needed
steps.tip: Start with less salt - you can always add more, but you cant remove it

steps.action: Remove from heat and stir in chopped parsley
steps.donenessCue: Parsley is evenly distributed and still bright green
steps.tip: Fresh parsley adds brightness and color - add at the end to preserve freshness
